From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

[![logo](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/99/Wiktionary-logo-en-v2.svg/40px-Wiktionary-logo-en-v2.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Wiktionary-logo-en-v2.svg)

Look up ***[twitter](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/twitter "wiktionary:twitter")*** or ***[Twitter](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/Twitter "wiktionary:Twitter")*** in Wiktionary, the free dictionary.

**[Twitter](/wiki/Twitter "Twitter")** is the former name of the social network X.

**Twitter** may also refer to:

* [History of Twitter](/wiki/History_of_Twitter "History of Twitter"), covering the platform's founding/creation in 2006, growth, initial public offering, and transition under new ownership
* [Tweet (social media)](/wiki/Tweet_(social_media) "Tweet (social media)"), the core post format on the platform
* [Twitter, Inc.](/wiki/Twitter,_Inc. "Twitter, Inc."), the former parent company of the social network until its merger with X Corp.
* [Twitter under Elon Musk](/wiki/Twitter_under_Elon_Musk "Twitter under Elon Musk"), the period of changes, controversies, and developments on the platform, including the rebrand to X, following Elon Musk's ownership
  + [Acquisition of Twitter by Elon Musk](/wiki/Acquisition_of_Twitter_by_Elon_Musk "Acquisition of Twitter by Elon Musk"), the 2022 purchase process that led to the platform's privatization and eventual rebranding
  + [Twitter Files](/wiki/Twitter_Files "Twitter Files"), a series of releases of select internal Twitter documents published from December 2022 through March 2023 on Twitter
